# billing

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> TypeSpec schema-only package defining the v1 billing API surface — billing profiles, customer overrides, and invoice lifecycle endpoints. All .tsp files compile into api/openapi.yaml via `make gen-api`; no runtime code lives here.

## Patterns

**@friendlyName on every model, union, enum, and interface** — Every public declaration carries @friendlyName to produce stable, human-readable Go type names. Missing @friendlyName causes autogenerated names that break SDK contracts on schema change. (`@friendlyName("BillingProfile")
model BillingProfile { ... }`)
**@visibility lifecycle decorators on every field** — Fields must be annotated with Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update as appropriate. Fields without @visibility default to all lifecycles and expose internal state in create/update payloads. (`@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)
supplier: BillingParty;`)
**Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic types** — All union variants use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) so the discriminator field is inlined. Adding a new union arm requires both the arm in the union AND a model with a matching `type` literal field. (`@discriminated(#{ discriminatorPropertyName: "type", envelope: "none" })
union BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignment {
  subscription: BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignmentSubscription,
  anchored: BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignmentAnchored,
}`)
**Spread (...) and OmitProperties<T> for model composition, not extends** — Models reuse shared fields via spread (e.g. ...global.Resource, ...OmitProperties<T, ...>). Using extends where spread is expected changes property semantics in generated SDK types. (`model BillingProfileCustomerOverride {
  ...OmitProperties<ResourceTimestamps, "deletedAt">;
  billingProfileId?: ULID;
}`)
**Explicit @operationId on every interface operation** — Every operation must carry @operationId to produce stable Go function names in the generated server stub. Omitting it causes autogenerated names that may change across TypeSpec compiler versions. (`@get
@operationId("getBillingProfile")
get(@path id: ULID): BillingProfile | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;`)
**namespace OpenMeter on all declarations** — Every model, union, enum, and interface must be declared inside namespace OpenMeter. Missing namespace causes the symbol to land in the global scope and fail to compile as part of the package. (`namespace OpenMeter;

@friendlyName("BillingWorkflow")
model BillingWorkflow { ... }`)
**main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files** — Every new .tsp file added to this folder must be imported in main.tsp. A file not imported there compiles in isolation but produces no output in api/openapi.yaml. (`// main.tsp
import "./invoices/main.tsp";
import "./profile.tsp";
import "./customeroverride.tsp";
import "./invoices.tsp";`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `main.tsp` | Package entry point: imports all sibling and child .tsp files and declares namespace usage directives. | Adding a new .tsp file without a matching import here — the file silently produces no output. |
| `profile.tsp` | Defines BillingProfile, BillingProfileCreate, BillingProfileReplaceUpdate, BillingWorkflow and its sub-settings (collection, invoicing, payment, tax), app references, and CRUD interface for /api/v1/billing/profiles. | BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> — adding a field to BillingProfile without controlling its visibility on the replace model will leak read-only fields into the update payload. |
| `customeroverride.tsp` | Defines customer override CRUD at /api/v1/billing/customers/{customerId} and BillingProfileCustomerOverrideWithDetails response merging override + base profile. | BillingProfileCustomerWorkflowOverride has app fields annotated @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — they must never appear in create/update inputs. |
| `invoices.tsp` | Defines invoice listing/get/delete/update/action endpoints across three interface groups (InvoicesEndpoints, InvoiceEndpoints, CustomerInvoiceEndpoints) and request/response models for invoice actions (void, approve, advance, retry, snapshot-quantities). | VoidInvoiceLineAction is a discriminated union with envelope:none — adding a new void action type requires both a new union arm AND a new model with a matching `type` literal field. |
| `invoices/main.tsp` | Child package entry: imports all invoice sub-models (invoice.tsp, discounts.tsp, party.tsp, pay.tsp, tax.tsp, docref.tsp). Invoice entity field shape lives in the child package, not the parent invoices.tsp. | Do not define invoice entity fields in the parent invoices.tsp — that file only defines endpoints and action input/output models. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Adding runtime or business logic — this folder is schema-only; all logic belongs in openmeter/billing/
- Editing generated files (api/openapi.yaml, api/api.gen.go) instead of the .tsp source
- Omitting @friendlyName on new models, unions, or enums — causes unstable generated Go type names that break SDK contracts
- Adding fields without @visibility decorators — fields default to all lifecycles and expose internal state on create/update requests
- Using extends for model composition instead of spread (...) or OmitProperties — changes property inheritance semantics in generated output

## Decisions

- **Visibility-controlled fields instead of separate request/response types** — Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Read/Create/Update) on a single model reduces duplication and ensures the OpenAPI generator derives correct request/response schemas automatically without maintaining parallel type hierarchies.
- **Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic workflow alignment and void-action types** — envelope:none inlines the discriminator field directly in the object, matching the Go domain types in openmeter/billing/ which use a Type string field rather than a wrapper object.
- **BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile> with an explicit workflow override field** — TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceReplaceModel auto-derives update payload from read model visibility; the explicit workflow field override allows different update semantics for workflow sub-config without duplicating the whole BillingProfile tree.

## Example: Add a new billing workflow action endpoint with a discriminated input type

```
// In invoices.tsp, inside InvoiceEndpoints:
@post
@route("/my-action")
@summary("Perform my action")
@operationId("myInvoiceAction")
myAction(
  @path invoiceId: ULID,
  @body request: MyActionInput,
): Invoice | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;

// New models (namespace OpenMeter already declared at top):
@friendlyName("MyActionType")
enum MyActionType { discard: "discard", redo: "redo" }

@friendlyName("MyActionInput")
// ...
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
